debbies-special-cranberry-sauce.name: Debbie's Special Cranberry Sauce
instructions: Combine the cranberries, prunes, water, raspberry jam, sugar, vinegar, salt, and cinnamon stick in a 3-quart saucepan; bring to a boil, stirring occasionally. Reduce heat to medium and simmer until thick and most of the cranberries have burst, about 10 minutes. Remove from heat and allow to cool. Transfer to a container with a lid; cover and refrigerate at least 3 hours before serving.

delicious-sweet-potato-pie.name: Delicious Sweet Potato Pie
instructions: Place sweet potatoes into a large pot and cover with water; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until tender, 10 to 15 minutes. Drain well and let cool.
Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line the bottom of a pie plate with pie crust.
Mash sweet potatoes with a potato masher or fork. Measure 2 cups of the mash into a large bowl. Mix in sugar eggs, butter, and vanilla extract.
Mix flour, pumpkin pie spice, and salt together in a separate bowl. Add to the sweet potato mixture.
Combine buttermilk and baking soda in a small bowl. Add to the sweet potato-flour mixture. Stir batter well; pour into the prepared crust.
Bake in the preheated oven until set, about 1 hour 10 minutes. Let cool on rack until firm, at least 20 minutes.

delicious-vanilla-ice-cream-comedera.name: Delicious Vanilla Ice Cream
instructions: A knife removes the whole inside of the vanilla bin.
Gather in a pot milk, four egg yolks and the vanilla extracted from the pod.
Mix very well and cook with low heat till thick and creamy consistency. Once you have that texture, turn off and reserve.
Incorporates cream and sugar into a bowl, mix very well until assembled and well grown.
Swallow the milk, egg and vanilla mixture into the cream.
Mix very well, leads to a package with cover and refrigerates for about 5 hours until you have a firm and creamy consistency.
Serve and come with your favorite topping.
Enjoy!

deliciously-sweet-salad-with-maple-nuts-.name: Deliciously Sweet Salad With Maple, Nuts, Seeds, Blueberries, And Goat Cheese
instructions: Toss romaine lettuce, blueberries, dried cranberries, sunflower seeds, walnut pieces, feta, and goat cheese in a large bowl. Pour vinegar, syrup, and oil over the salad one at a time; toss until evenly coated. Season with salt.

dirty-fried-rice.name: Dirty Fried Rice

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
Thai crab or shrimp paste with bean oil

instructions: Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season chicken livers with salt and pepper and cook until browned on the outside but still pink inside, about 1½ minutes per side. Transfer to a large plate and let cool. Pulse in a food processor to a coarse paste; set chicken liver paste aside.
Heat remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in same skillet over medium-high heat and cook onion, celery, chile, garlic, and ginger, scraping up any browned bits, until vegetables are tender, about 2 minutes.

Add soy sauce, vinegar, hot sauce, crab paste, reserved chicken liver paste, and ¼ cup water to skillet. Reduce heat to medium and cook, stirring occasionally, until a thick dry paste forms. (You should have about ½ cup.)
DO AHEAD: Base can be made 1 day ahead. Let cool; cover and chill.

Heat oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat. Cook eggs, stirring constantly, until fluffy and just set, about 1 minute. Add rice and cook, stirring often, until rice is evenly coated and beginning to brown, about 4 minutes.
Add shrimp and chicken-liver base and stir to coat. Cook, stirring often, until shrimp is cooked through, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in jalapeño.
Serve fried rice topped with scallions, Old Bay seasoning, and MSG, if using, and drizzled with chili oil.

dragans-leg-of-lamb-with-garlic-and-beer.name: Dragan's Leg Of Lamb With Garlic And Beer
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add potatoes and carrots; cook in boiling water for about 3 minutes. Drain and set aside.

Rinse leg of lamb, pat dry, and place in a roasting pan. Season generously with salt and pepper; rub into meat. Use a small knife to make incisions big enough for a garlic clove to fit all over lamb. Stuff garlic cloves into the holes. Spoon mustard over lamb; rub into meat.

Roast, uncovered, in the preheated oven for about 30 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 191°C (190 degrees C). Add potatoes and carrots to the roasting pan. Baste lamb with beer, reserving the rest for basting at 20-minute intervals.
Continue to roast lamb until the internal temperature reaches at least 60°C (60 degrees C) for medium rare, about 1 hour 30 minutes more.
Remove from the oven and allow to rest for at least 5 minutes before carving. Spoon pan drippings over meat and vegetables to serve.

dusted-onion-chicken.name: Dusted Onion Chicken
instructions: Season chicken breasts with salt and pepper, then dredge breasts through flour, egg, then Panko breadcrumbs to create
Wrap and rest in the fridge for 15 minutes.
Add an inch of oil or more and heat oil to approx 177°C. Cook each side of the chicken until the internal temperature reaches 77°C for proper doneness.
Take thinly sliced onions and dust them with seasoned flour. Add to hot oil and fry well, then top chicken with the onions.
Serve with a salad, gravy, and/or vegetables.
